answer:
**Manusmṛti 5.155 is not present in the Falsafa corpus version of the text.**

The available *Manusmṛti* (*unknown-manusmrti-347b76*) has 12 chapters in verse layout.[^1] Chapter 5 contains 106 paragraphs (corresponding to verses), labeled from Mn_4.196 (opening spillover) through at least Mn_5.41 (closing excerpt), with no Mn_5.155 or equivalent verse found via direct search or full chapter read.[^2][^3] Searches for the verse reference ("Manu 5.155", "5.155", "Mn_5.155") across the English translation yield zero results.[^4]

Chapter 5 primarily prescribes dietary rules, forbidden foods, and meat-eating norms for twice-born men (e.g., avoiding food touched by a menstruating woman or from an unchaste woman), but contains no verse 155 or direct prescription *for* women.[^5][^6] Rules concerning women appear elsewhere, such as Chapter 9 (on marriage, duties, inheritance).[^7]

If this refers to a different edition or numbering, it is not covered here. The corpus has 37 works; not every verse or topic is included.[^8]

[^1]: *Manusmṛti*, metadata via `list_chapters("unknown-manusmrti-347b76")`[/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/05/translation/].
[^2]: *Manusmṛti* ch. 5 wiki card[/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/wiki/05.card.md].
[^3]: *Manusmṛti* ch. 5 full chapter (`read_chapter`, translation variant)[/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/05/translation/].
[^4]: `search_corpus("5.155")`, `"Mn_5.155"`, etc., 0 results.
[^5]: [p-058068] "nor touched by a menstruating woman" (Mn_4.208)[/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/05/translation/#p-058068].
[^6]: [p-4e57e1] "of an unchaste woman" (Mn_4.211)[/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/05/translation/#p-4e57e1].
[^7]: *Manusmṛti* ch. 9 wiki card[/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/wiki/09.card.md].
[^8]: Corpus overview via `list_works`.
